title: Theories of leadership
summary: 9609 A Level — trait, behavioural, situational, and transformational leadership theories applied to cases.
learningObjectives: Explain trait, behavioural, situational, and transformational theories.
Apply situational theory to match style to context.

simpleExplanation.title: Explaining effective leadership
simpleExplanation.summary: Trait theory says leaders are born with qualities; behavioural theory focuses on task vs people styles; situational theory matches style to the situation; transformational leaders inspire change beyond transactions.
steps: Trait: innate qualities — confidence, intelligence (limited proof).
Behavioural: Ohio/Michigan — task vs relationship focus.
Situational: Hersey-Blanchard — adapt to follower readiness.

flashcards.back: Leaders born with personality traits — hard to train, oversimplified.
No single best style — depends on team maturity and task.
Inspire change via vision, role model, individual consideration.
Rewards/punishments for meeting targets — contrast with transformational.
Name theory → explain → apply to case leader → evaluate limitation.

sections.content: **Theories of leadership** (7.3.2) — **situational** and **transformational** appear most in Paper 3/4. Always **apply to named case leader**, not abstract CEOs.

items: **Trait:** Simple but ignores training; can't explain why some fail.
**Behavioural:** Shows style can be learned; ignores context.
**Situational:** Flexible — new team needs directing; expert team needs delegating.
**Transformational:** Strong for change; risk of dependency on one charismatic leader.

sections.question: New plant manager joins failing factory with demoralised, inexperienced team. Which leadership approach fits and why?
sections.solution:
**Situational analysis:** Team **low competence + low confidence** → need **high task, high relationship** (selling/coaching style).

**Not laissez-faire:** Would **worsen chaos**.

**Not pure transformational yet:** Vision meaningless without **basic discipline** first.

**Behavioural:** **Clear targets**, **daily floor presence** (task) + **team meetings**, **recognition** (people).

**Later:** As skills rise, **delegate** more — situational shift to **supporting** style.
